paper_title: ESTIMATION OF FAMILY LABOR SUPPLY: EVIDENCE FROM THE MEKONG RIVER DELTA IN VIET NAM

entities.id: ESTIMATION OF FAMILY LABOR SUPPLY: EVIDENCE FROM THE MEKONG RIVER DELTA IN VIET NAM
entities.description: The title and primary research focus regarding familial labor allocation in Vietnam.
The central concept exploring how families allocate labor in response to market wage changes.
The theoretical framework used to examine hypotheses on family labor supply.
An exogenous factor in the labor supply function estimated from market wages.
A labor supply curve where supply increases in parallel with wage rises.
Instrumental Variable methodology used to obtain consistent estimations and address endogeneity.
Ordinary Least Squares methodology used to estimate coefficients of the labor supply equation.

entities.id: Viet Nam Household Living Standards Survey 2008 (VHLSS 2008)
entities.description: The digital survey data source conducted by the GSO and World Bank.
The specific number of family units extracted from the VHLSS 2008 for analysis.
The recorded effect size of shadow wage changes interpreted by observed wage variation.
The percentage of the total population in the MRD living in rural areas based on 2008 data.
The broader field of research, specifically labor and development economics.
The researcher from Can Tho University who authored the article.

entities.id: College of Economics, Can Tho University
entities.description: The academic institution in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.

relations.source: ESTIMATION OF FAMILY LABOR SUPPLY: EVIDENCE FROM THE MEKONG RIVER DELTA IN VIET NAM
